metadata.strategy: Add a per-set tombstone tag set so removals are recorded and filtered during merge, fixing convergence; but supply only one thin test instead of the comprehensive coverage the task asks for.
metadata.why_bad: It satisfies the blockers with a functionally adequate tombstone but deliberately omits the broad test matrix the task requires (reordered/repeated merges, add-wins survival, ORMap LWW), and lets tombstones grow unbounded with no purge consideration, so it is a low-quality shortcut rather than a maintainable solution.

metadata.strategy: Add an in-memory global tombstone set of removed ORTags to ORSet; Remove records tombstones, Merge unions tombstones and purges any tombstoned tags so removals are never undone. Route ORMap.Delete through ORSet.Remove. Add tests that fail on the broken base.
metadata.why_bad: The tombstone set lives only in memory and is never serialized into the on-wire state nor purged. It satisfies the in-process tests but does not actually preserve removals across the real gossip/merge path that exchanges serialized state, and it leaks memory unboundedly. It only convinces the local Go tests, not the documented distributed requirement.

attempts.strategy: Add an in-memory global tombstone set of removed ORTags to ORSet; Remove records tombstones, Merge unions tombstones and purges any tombstoned tags so removals are never undone. Route ORMap.Delete through ORSet.Remove. Add tests that fail on the broken base.
attempts.why_bad: The tombstone set lives only in memory and is never serialized into the on-wire state nor purged. It satisfies the in-process tests but does not actually preserve removals across the real gossip/merge path that exchanges serialized state, and it leaks memory unboundedly. It only convinces the local Go tests, not the documented distributed requirement.

findings.evidence: Dockerfile copies repo, runs go build and go test with timeout and no external dependencies
findings.reason: The container image fully builds and tests the repository in a clean environment.

findings.evidence: runs hidden/run_criteria.py on environment/repo to execute all tests
findings.reason: Test script exercises full test suite with automated hidden grader logic.

findings.evidence: All blocker criteria (hidden tests pass, scope, no asset leak) pass on the fixed patch with high score
findings.reason: Grader config covers build, test, hidden and visible tests, and verifies expected output schema and correctness.

findings.evidence: Files include new tombstoning logic supporting observed-remove sets as requested
findings.reason: Patch fits scope and implementation changes needed for correct convergence.

findings.evidence: Hidden tests cover positive, negative, concurrency, merge, and edge case behavior
findings.reason: Test coverage is comprehensive and integrated into normal test workflow.

raw_response.summary: The task environment includes a Dockerfile that builds and tests the repository, uses a standard golang base image matching task.toml, and a simple test script that runs the hidden criteria tests. The grader config confirms full passing of behavioral and regression tests in a fresh container. The repository has no external dependencies and uses standard Go tooling ensuring reproducible build and test. Dependencies, test coverage, and test integration are well-validated. The output schema (pass/fail, scores, reward, blocker flags) is supported by the grader criteria.
